content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game on a sequence of **N** positive integers, say **A1, A2, ... AN**. The game is played as follows.

If all the numbers are equal, the game ends.
    Otherwise
        Select two numbers which are unequal
            Subtract the smaller number from the larger number
            Replace the larger number with the result from above (see the explanation section for clarity)
        
Chef has already figured out that the game **always terminates**. He also knows, for a given sequence of integers, the game will always terminate on the **same value**, no matter how the game is played. Chef wants you to simulate the game for him and tell him on which value will the game terminate for a given sequence of integers.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the sequence. The second line contains N positive integers A1, A2, ..., AN separated by single spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the value on which all numbers become equal after the game ends.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case the GCD of 10 and 12 is 2, so the game ends with all numbers equal to 2. For the second test case the GCD of 5, 9 and 4 is 1.
The GCD of 2, 4, 6 and 8 is 2, therefore the final equal value is 2.
All numbers are already equal; the GCD is 3.

solution.approach:
The operation described is exactly the Euclidean algorithm applied pair‑wise. Replacing the larger number by the difference of the two numbers does not change the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the whole multiset. Repeating the operation until all numbers become equal therefore forces every number to become the GCD of the initial set. Hence the answer for each test case is simply gcd(A1, A2, ..., AN). Compute the GCD iteratively using the Euclidean algorithm.

Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. For each test case:
   a. Read N and the array.
   b. Initialise ans = A1.
   c. For i = 2 .. N: ans = gcd(ans, Ai).
   d. Print ans.
The Euclidean algorithm runs in O(log max(Ai)) time, so the whole solution is O(T·N·log maxAi) time and O(1) extra space.
